title: Sound and Waves
intro_hook: In space no one can hear you scream. Why not? What exactly IS sound?
big_idea: Sound is mechanical vibration traveling through a medium. Frequency determines pitch. Amplitude determines loudness. Sound cannot travel through vacuum.

cause_process_result.cause: Object vibrates (speaker, vocal cords, guitar string)
cause_process_result.process: Molecules bump into neighbors in a chain â€” compression and rarefaction waves propagate
cause_process_result.result: Wave reaches ear; eardrum vibrates; brain interprets as sound

Key concepts: Frequency and Pitch, Amplitude and Volume, Wave Properties, Sound Speed

Dogs hear up to 65,000 Hz â€” dog whistles work above human range of 20,000 Hz

misconceptions.belief: Sound can travel through space (like in movies)
misconceptions.probe: Krakatoa 1883 explosion was heard 3,000 miles away on Earth but not in space. Why?
misconceptions.repair: Sound is mechanical â€” it needs molecules to travel. Space is vacuum with no molecules. Movie space explosions are scientifically wrong.

misconceptions.probe: Does a shout travel faster than a whisper?
misconceptions.repair: Speed of sound is fixed by the medium (~343 m/s in air at 20 deg C). Amplitude affects energy and loudness â€” not speed.

question_flow.q: In space no one can hear you scream. Why not? What exactly IS sound?
What is the most surprising thing about this concept?
Trace through: Object vibrates (speaker, vocal cords, guitar string) â€” what happens step by step?
question_flow.a: Object vibrate hone par, wo vibration hawa mein sound waves ke roop mein failati hai, jisse hume dhvani sunai deti hai, jaise ki speaker, vocal cords, ya guitar string mein vibration hone par sound waves generate hoti hai aur hume sunai deti hai. Is prakriya mein, vibration se sound waves banate hai aur phir hume sunai deti hai.

question_flow.q: Ab tu mujhe bata â€” apne words mein. Koi analogy use karo.
Krakatoa 1883 explosion was heard 3,000 miles away on Earth but not in space. Why?
Does a shout travel faster than a whisper?

indian_legacy.connection: The ancient Indian sage Kanada, also known as the founder of the Vaisheshika school of philosophy, explained the concept of sound and vibrations in his works. He described sound as a vibration that travels through a medium, such as air, and discussed the idea that the frequency and amplitude of these vibrations determine the pitch and loudness of the sound, respectively.
indian_legacy.story_hook: Imagine being in a bustling Indian market, surrounded by the sounds of vendors calling out and instruments playing, when you stumble upon an ancient text that reveals the secrets of sound as understood by Sage Kanada. As you delve into the text, you begin to uncover the fascinating science behind the sounds that fill your surroundings, and how they have been understood and explained by Indian scholars for centuries.
